seo.title: Building Code Generation with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: AutoGen enables powerful multi-agent systems for code generation, where agents collaborate to write, test, and refine code iteratively. However, without proper guardrails, these systems risk infinite loops, unsafe code execution, and resource exhaustion. Vex integration provides essential verification to ensure safe, reliable code generation workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The typical AutoGen code generation architecture features a CodeGenerator agent that produces code snippets based on user requirements, a CodeExecutor agent that runs the code in a sandboxed environment, and a Tester agent that validates outputs against test cases. These agents communicate via a group chat manager, enabling iterative refinement through feedback loops. Human oversight can be injected at key decision points.

The workflow begins with a user request routed to the CodeGenerator, which crafts initial code. The CodeExecutor runs it safely and returns results or errors. The Tester validates functionality, security, and performance, feeding issues back for regeneration. This closed-loop system excels at complex coding tasks but amplifies risks like malicious code injection and runaway conversations.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Request
      ↓
[GroupChatManager]
  ↙       ↘
CodeGenerator  ←→ CodeExecutor → Tester
  ↓                    ↓
Code Drafts     Sandbox Results  Test Verdict
  ↑____________________________↑
           Iterative Refinement

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents continuously debate code improvements without convergence criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces max-turn limits and convergence scoring on replies

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: Generated code executes system commands, network calls, or file modifications
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code for dangerous APIs and enforces sandbox policies

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Multiple agents validate flawed code due to shared hallucination
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-verifies code syntax, safety, and test execution independently

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.description: Verbose agent discussions exhaust budget without progress
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage per turn and enforces conversation budgets

implementation.title: Set up AutoGen Agents for Code Generation
implementation.description: Create specialized agents for code generation, execution, and testing with group chat orchestration
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

config_list = [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "your-key"}]

coder = AssistantAgent(
    name="Coder",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    system_message="You generate Python code. Always include tests."
)
executor = AssistantAgent(
    name="Executor",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    system_message="Execute code safely and report results. Never run unsafe code."
)
tester = AssistantAgent(
    name="Tester",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    system_message="Validate code correctness, safety, and efficiency."
)
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(name="User")

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[user_proxy, coder, executor, tester], messages=[])
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"config_list": config_list})

implementation.title: Implement Sandboxed Code Execution
implementation.description: Use restricted execution environment to safely run generated code
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def safe_execute(code: str) -> dict:
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.py', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(code)
        temp_file = f.name
    
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['python', temp_file],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=30,
            env={**os.environ, 'PYTHONPATH': ''}
        )
        return {
            'success': result.returncode == 0,
            'stdout': result.stdout,
            'stderr': result.stderr
        }
    finally:
        os.unlink(temp_file)

implementation.title: Basic Code Safety Pre-checks
implementation.description: Static analysis before execution to catch obvious dangers

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Filter
vexIntegration.description: Install and configure Vex SDK to filter all agent replies for safety
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Register Vex as reply function filter for all agents
@vex.register_filter(policies=[
    "code-safety",
    "no-credential-leakage",
    "syntax-validity",
    "dependency-safety"
])
def vex_reply_filter(message: str, agent_name: str, context: dict) -> bool:
    """Vex verifies code in agent replies before forwarding"""
    verification = vex_client.verify({
        "content": message,
        "context": {
            "agent": agent_name,
            "task_type": "code-generation",
            "turn_count": context.get('turn_count', 0)
        },
        "policies": [
            "safe-python-code",
            "no-network-access",
            "no-file-modification",
            "syntax-valid",
            "no-hardcoded-secrets"
        ]
    })
    return verification.passed

# Apply to AutoGen agents
coder.register_reply(coder.generate_reply, reply_filter=vex_reply_filter)
executor.register_reply(executor.generate_reply, reply_filter=vex_reply_filter)
tester.register_reply(tester.generate_reply, reply_filter=vex_reply_filter)
